Want to Run Your Own LLM? Understanding Model Parameters and Quantization

If you've ever tried downloading an AI model to run on your own machine, you've probably hit Hugging Face and seen 7B, Q4_K_M, GGUF — a wall of codes with no clue which one your computer can handle. This post unpacks what those codes actually mean and how to pick a model that fits your hardware.
AI Agent Isn't One Thing Anymore — Understanding Coding Agents and Other AI Agent Types

Back when I wrote about AI terminology, AI Agent was used as a catch-all term for systems that can understand a task on their own, break it into steps, and use tools to get things done. But here in 2026, the term AI Agent has started to splinter into more specific subcategories — and the most mature one is the Coding Agent (some people call it Code Agent). This post walks through what these categories actually mean and how they differ.
Is Your Claude Code Context Getting Eaten Alive? AGENTS.md and the Complete Context Management Guide

This post is about Claude Code's Context management problem. A lot of people install a bunch of Skills, Plugins, and MCP Servers, then write a giant CLAUDE.md — and before they've even started working, half their Context is already gone. We'll also cover AGENTS.md (a cross-tool universal standard) and how to make sure your Context goes where it actually matters.
Is SEO Not Enough? Meet AEO — Getting Your Site Found by AI Search

This post is a quick walk through AEO (Answer Engine Optimization). With more and more people swapping Google for ChatGPT and Perplexity, the old SEO playbook needs an update — so let's talk about what AEO actually is, how it differs from SEO, and what you can do about it.
Which Claude Code Files Should You Commit? CLAUDE.md, Settings, Rules, Skills — A Complete Breakdown

After you start using Claude Code, your project ends up with a bunch of new files — CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json, rules, skills, and so on. Which ones should you commit? Which are personal settings that shouldn't be pushed up? This post walks through Claude Code's multi-tier settings architecture and load order, so you understand the line between personal and team.
What Is Claude Code Auto Memory? A Complete Guide to the Auto Memory Feature

Tired of having to re-explain your preferences every time you open a new Claude Code conversation? Claude Code actually has an Auto Memory feature that automatically jots down your corrections and preferences during conversations, so you don't have to repeat yourself next time. This post walks through how Auto Memory works, where it's stored, how it gets loaded, and how to manage it.
Claude Code Status Line Setup: Monitor Context Usage and Rate Limit in Real-Time

Tired of finding out your Context got compacted or your Rate Limit got eaten through only after the fact? This post walks through Claude Code's Status Line feature — showing model, remaining context, and rate limit usage live at the bottom of your terminal — plus a customised script with a progress bar and a three-stage colour warning. Copy, paste, done.
Which AI Coding Tool Should You Pick in 2026? Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code vs Kiro vs Antigravity

AI Coding tools are everywhere in 2026 — GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Kiro, and Google Antigravity. Which one should you actually pick? This post compares all five across features, pricing, real-world use cases, and Agent capabilities so you can find the one that fits your workflow.
Don't Want to Use the Lobster (OpenClaw)? Try Claude Code Channels Instead!

Have you been using OpenClaw (the "lobster") for remote computer control? Claude Code actually has a similar feature called "Channels" that lets you push messages from Telegram or Discord into a running Claude Code Session for Claude to handle. This post walks through what Channels are, how to set them up, and real-world use cases.
Claude Cowork Dispatch and Remote Control — Control Your AI Agent From Your Phone

Ever thought about controlling the AI Agent on your computer from your phone? Anthropic recently launched two big features — "Dispatch" and "Remote Control" — that let you remotely control Claude Cowork and Claude Code from your phone. Even when you're away from your computer, you can assign tasks, monitor progress, and come back to a finished result. This post walks through the differences between the two features, how to set them up, and real-world use cases.
